title: Mrs Tremblay's path first
summary: In Quebec, a snow-clearer with a small truck always begins his round at the same house.
body:
Pierre lives in a small town in Quebec. He has an old truck with a plough on the front. When it snows, he clears paths and driveways.

It snows a lot in Quebec. Sometimes thirty centimetres in one night. People sleep, and the snow falls. In the morning, they cannot open the door.

Pierre has many customers. They pay him each month. He has a list. The list has twenty-three names. On the list, there are houses, two shops, and a small church.

But every morning, before the list, Pierre goes to one house first. It is the house of Mrs Tremblay. She is eighty-six. She lives alone. Her husband died eleven years ago. Her son lives in Montreal, far away.

Mrs Tremblay does not pay Pierre. She is not on the list. He clears her path for free. He clears it before the sun comes up.

Why?

When Pierre was a child, his mother was sick for a long winter. The family had no money. A neighbour cleared their path every morning. He never asked for anything. Pierre's mother got better in the spring.

That neighbour was Mr Tremblay.

Mr Tremblay is gone. Mrs Tremblay is still here. So Pierre starts at her house. Every storm. Before the list. Quietly. While she is still sleeping.

She does not always know. But the path is clear.

annotations.meaningVi: lưỡi cày / lưỡi gạt tuyết (gắn trước xe)
đã đi / đã mất (here: đã mất)
lặng lẽ / không gây tiếng động

grammarNotes.title: Adverbs of time at the start of a sentence
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong tiếng Anh, có thể đặt cụm chỉ thời gian ở đầu câu để làm rõ khi nào việc xảy ra. Trong bài: 'When it snows, he clears paths and driveways.' 'In the morning, they cannot open the door.' 'Every morning, before the list, Pierre goes to one house first.' Sau cụm thời gian thường có dấu phẩy. Lưu ý 'When' ở đây mở đầu mệnh đề thời gian với hiện tại đơn để nói về sự việc lặp lại.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Time phrases can open a sentence to make the time clear. In the passage: 'When it snows, he clears paths and driveways.' 'In the morning, they cannot open the door.' 'Every morning, before the list, Pierre goes to one house first.' A comma usually follows the time phrase. Note that 'When' here opens a time clause in the present simple to describe a repeated event.

translationVi:
Pierre sống ở một thị trấn nhỏ tại Quebec. Ông có một chiếc xe tải cũ với lưỡi gạt tuyết gắn phía trước. Khi tuyết rơi, ông dọn các lối đi và lối xe vào nhà.

Ở Quebec tuyết rơi nhiều. Có khi ba mươi xentimét trong một đêm. Người ta ngủ, và tuyết rơi. Buổi sáng, họ không mở được cửa.

Pierre có nhiều khách. Họ trả tiền theo tháng. Ông có một danh sách. Trên đó có hai mươi ba cái tên. Trong danh sách có nhà, hai cửa hàng, và một nhà thờ nhỏ.

Nhưng mỗi sáng, trước khi làm theo danh sách, Pierre đến một nhà trước. Đó là nhà bà Tremblay. Bà tám mươi sáu tuổi. Bà sống một mình. Chồng bà mất mười một năm trước. Con trai bà sống ở Montreal, rất xa.

Bà Tremblay không trả tiền cho Pierre. Bà không có trong danh sách. Ông dọn lối nhà bà miễn phí. Ông dọn trước khi mặt trời mọc.

Tại sao?

Hồi Pierre còn bé, mẹ ông ốm suốt một mùa đông dài. Gia đình không có tiền. Một người hàng xóm dọn lối nhà họ mỗi sáng. Ông ấy không bao giờ đòi gì. Mùa xuân, mẹ Pierre khoẻ lại.

Người hàng xóm đó là ông Tremblay.

Ông Tremblay đã mất. Bà Tremblay vẫn còn đây. Vậy nên Pierre bắt đầu từ nhà bà. Mỗi cơn bão tuyết. Trước danh sách. Lặng lẽ. Khi bà còn đang ngủ.

Bà không phải lúc nào cũng biết. Nhưng lối đi đã sạch.

questions.prompt: What does Pierre do when it snows?
questions.explanation: 'When it snows, he clears paths and driveways.'

questions.prompt: How many names are on his list?
Whose house does Pierre clear first, every morning?
questions.explanation: 'It is the house of Mrs Tremblay.'
'Her son lives in Montreal, far away.'

options: No, he clears her path for free
questions.explanation: 'She is not on the list. He clears her path for free.'

questions.prompt: What happened to Pierre's family when he was a child?
options: His mother was sick for a long winter and the family had no money
questions.explanation: 'When Pierre was a child, his mother was sick for a long winter. The family had no money.'

questions.prompt: Who cleared their path back then?
Why does Pierre start at Mrs Tremblay's house?
options: To repay an old kindness her husband gave his family
questions.explanation: The story links Mr Tremblay's past help to Pierre's present action.

options: A kindness can travel forward through many years, even without words.
Old people should move to the city.
questions.explanation: The ending — quiet repayment while she sleeps — supports this.

It snows a lot in Quebec. Sometimes thirty {{b2}} in one night. People sleep, and the snow falls. In the morning, they cannot open the door.

Mrs Tremblay does not pay Pierre. She is not on the list. He clears her path for {{b6}}. He clears it before the sun comes up.

When Pierre was a child, his mother was {{b7}} for a long winter. The family had no money. A neighbour cleared their path every morning. He never asked for anything. Pierre's mother got better in the spring.

Mr Tremblay is gone. Mrs Tremblay is still here. So Pierre starts at her house. Every {{b8}}. Before the list. {{b9}}. While she is still sleeping.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Pierre repays a kindness his family received decades ago, even though the person who helped is no longer alive. Is there a small, quiet kindness in your past that you have not yet repaid forward to someone else?
